objective: Measure whether the Wave 62 capacity-clearing left-supply source survives the value-unique selector and materialization path under a bounded candidate-pair budget.
non_negotiables: This is a bounded selector diagnostic and must not be treated as full rpf50 support.
The report must preserve value-unique selection rules.
Candidate-pair limits and limit-hit status must be recorded.
A low selected count after capacity clearing must be treated as a selector/materialization bottleneck, not as source capacity failure.

tasks.prompt: Add and run a value-unique bounded selector report for the Wave 62 unbounded expanded-plus-outer left-supply source.
outputs: rpf50 bounded selector report with candidate offsets and rejection counts
acceptance: The report uses the Wave 62 capacity-clearing source.
The report records selected rows and selected topology counts.
The report records candidate offsets and whether each topology hit the candidate-pair limit.
The report records value-unique rejection counts.

tasks.prompt: Record Wave 63 as a bounded selector bottleneck diagnostic and keep rpf50 support claims closed.
commands: python3 -m py_compile engine/ml_model.py engine/test.py agents/network.py agents/label_schema.py
acceptance: Docs state that the 2500-pair bounded selector selects 12 rows.
Docs state that every topology hits the candidate-pair limit.
Docs preserve the Wave 62 capacity-clearing result while blocking support promotion.
Docs route next work toward selector ordering, value-aware pairing, or materialization-aware search.

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/network.py validate && python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_63_left_supply_bounded_selector.json
integration_gates.success_signal: The Wave 63 plan is internally consistent.
The Wave 63 bounded-selector report is valid JSON.
